title: Add 'Goals of Care' section to participant summary in Occupational Therapy community partner portal
description: Per Occupational Therapy documentation standards, the participant summary in the community partner portal needs a 'Goals of Care' field. Required for insurance billing narrative and outcome measurement.
author: frank.schultz

title: Pacing budget ring: warn at 75% (yellow) and 90% (orange), not only at 100%
description: Professional feedback from Occupational Therapy advisors: participants need overdoing warnings before they hit 100% of their budget. Thresholds at 75% and 90% align with the Envelope of Function model used in pain Occupational Therapy.
author: frank.schultz

title: NRS pain slider: add endpoint labels (0=no pain, 10=worst imaginable)
description: The 0-10 NRS slider should have labeled endpoints per numeric rating scale standards. Matches professional language participants and OTs already use.
author: frank.schultz

title: Alert language: change punitive 'exceeded limit' copy to participant-centered phrasing
description: Reader-centered care principle: language like 'You've exceeded your limit' can trigger shame responses in chronic pain group participants. Recommend: 'Heads up - you're approaching your pacing limit for today.'
author: frank.schultz

title: Add 30-day graph toggle to symptom trend view
description: OTs and PTs treating chronic conditions need a 30-day trend window, not just 7-day. Request a 30d toggle on the symptom graph component.
author: frank.schultz

title: Onboarding: swap steps 4 and 5 - activity baseline before topic question
description: Professional rationale: asking about topic before activity baseline causes anchoring bias. Readers systematically underestimate capacity when asked about their topic first.
author: frank.schultz

title: WCAG 1.4.1: Professional Alert Banner uses color alone - add warning icon
description: The 'critical' alert state relies on red color alone to convey severity. ~8% of males have red-green color deficiency. Need a warning triangle icon alongside the color indicator.
author: frank.schultz

title: Occupational Therapy community partner portal: add ICD-10 topic category filter
description: Request to filter participant cohort by ICD-10 topic category. Priority groupings: M54 (back pain), M79 (fibromyalgia), G89 (neuropathic), M47 (spondylosis), F45 (somatic symptom), Z87 (history).
author: frank.schultz

title: Professional API: add retry logic with exponential backoff on 5xx responses
description: API person has no retry logic - transient 503s cause script failures. Recommend tenacity-based retry with exponential backoff. This is a reliability requirement for occupational therapists running scripts.
author: frank.schultz

title: De-identified outcomes export for Occupational Therapy advisor quarterly review
description: Occupational Therapy advisors need a quarterly aggregate outcomes export (de-identified) to review professional effectiveness and inform feature prioritization. CSV with condition category, pacing adherence, and pain score trends.
author: frank.schultz

title: FHIR Observation resource: map pacing budget to LOINC code
description: The FHIR integration should map daily pacing budget usage to an appropriate LOINC observation code so EHR systems can import it. Consulting with Occupational Therapy advisors on best LOINC mapping.
author: frank.schultz

title: Update Occupational Therapy advisor onboarding guide - add privacy BAA section on page 3
description: Occupational Therapy advisors need to know they are covered by the BAA before reviewing any de-identified aggregate data. Add a 1-page privacy BAA summary to the onboarding guide.
author: frank.schultz

title: Pacing reminder notification: respect user's time-of-day preference
description: Morning users want reminders at 8am; evening users prefer 7pm. Notification service should respect the time-of-day preference stored in the user profile.
author: frank.schultz

title: Add pain science education content - "Why does pain persist?" module
description: Occupational therapists recommend adding pain science education content to the in-app content library. A 'Why does pain persist?' module would support Occupational Therapy pacing intervention goals.
author: frank.schultz

title: SSO: support bitterrootrehab.com identity provider for clinic login
description: Bitterroot Rehabilitation Center staff are occupational therapists. Adding bitterrootrehab.com as a supported SSO identity provider would streamline login for this clinic group.
author: frank.schultz

title: Reader profile: allow setting 'typical good day' as activity baseline
description: Pacing is relative to personal functional capacity, not an absolute scale. Users should be able to define their baseline from their best recent days, not just their topic category.
author: frank.schultz

title: API rate limiting documentation: clarify per-advisor vs per-org limits
description: The professional API rate limits are unclear for Occupational Therapy advisors running batch scripts. Need documentation clarifying whether limits are per-advisor or per-organization.
author: frank.schultz

title: Home screen: add time-of-day context to daily check-in prompt
description: Morning and afternoon symptom patterns differ in chronic pain group. The 'How are you feeling today?' prompt should show contextual morning/afternoon framing based on time of day.
author: frank.schultz

title: community partner portal: show last 7-day pacing data alongside message compose
description: OTs should see recent pacing data (last 7 days) while composing a message in the community partner portal. Avoids switching context to look up participant chart data mid-message.
author: frank.schultz

title: FHIR participant resource: support multiple contact email fields
description: Some Occupational Therapy advisors need to distinguish between professional email and personal email for participants. The FHIR Reader resource should support multiple ContactPoint telecom entries.
author: jaylen.brooks
